Apple’s board of directors has declared a cash dividend of $0.27 per share of the Company’s common stock, an increase of 4 percent. The dividend is payable on May 14, 2026, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on May 11, 2026. The board of directors has also authorized an additional program to repurchase up to $100 billion of the Company’s common stock.
Apple will provide live streaming of its Q2 2026 financial results conference call beginning at 2:00 p.m. PT on April 30, 2026, at apple.com/investor/earnings-call. The webcast will be available for replay for approximately two weeks thereafter.

Apple periodically provides information for investors on its corporate website, apple.com, and its investor relations website, investor.apple.com. This includes press releases and other information about financial performance, reports filed or furnished with the SEC, information on corporate governance, and details related to its annual meeting of shareholders.

“Today Apple is proud to report our best March quarter ever, with revenue of $111.2 billion and double-digit growth across every geographic segment,” said Tim Cook, Apple’s CEO. “iPhone achieved a March quarter revenue record, fueled by such extraordinary demand for the iPhone 17 lineup. During the quarter, Services achieved yet another all-time record, and we were excited to introduce remarkable new products to our strongest lineup ever. That included the addition of the iPhone 17e and the M4-powered iPad Air, along with the launch of MacBook Neo, which is captivating customers all around the world.”

“Our strong business performance during the March quarter generated over $28 billion in operating cash flow and drove new March quarter records for both operating cash flow and EPS,” said Kevan Parekh, Apple’s CFO. “Continued strong customer demand for our products and services once again helped us achieve a new all-time high for our installed base of active devices across all major product categories and geographic segments.”

# Executive Summary  
   Apple Inc., headquartered in Cupertino, California, operates primarily within the technology sector, specializing in consumer electronics like iPhones, iPads, MacBooks, and Apple Watches. With robust profitability metrics including a net margin of 26.92%, return on equity (ROE) of 151.91%, and steady revenue growth of 6.43% annually, the company demonstrates consistent operational efficiency and market leadership. Despite recent challenges highlighted by declining free cash flows (-9.23%) and increasing leverage (1.34), the intrinsic value assessment places Apple at a premium compared to its enterprise value ($1393251460464.5356). Given positive overall sentiment and anticipated future dividends, there exists potential for upside given current valuations. However, caution should be exercised due to ongoing economic uncertainties affecting global markets. 

   # Bull Case  
   Apple continues to maintain solid profit margins despite facing external pressures. Recent earnings calls indicate continued investment in advanced technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), enhancing user experiences through innovative features like the iPhone 17 series and the introduction of the M4-powered iPad Air. These initiatives support long-term strategic goals while maintaining competitive advantage. Additionally, the company's commitment to shareholder returns via regular dividend payments adds stability to its financial profile. Furthermore, the growing popularity of Apple products globally underscores sustained demand for their offerings. Thus, the bullish case hinges on these factors reinforcing Apple's position as a leading player in tech innovation and consumer electronics.

   # Bear Case  
   While Apple maintains significant strengths, concerns arise regarding its ability to sustain growth amidst evolving technological landscapes and shifting consumer preferences. Declining free cash flows coupled with rising debt levels suggest vulnerabilities in liquidity management. Moreover, geopolitical tensions could disrupt supply chains crucial for manufacturing operations. Consequently, the bearish scenario posits risks associated with reduced operational flexibility and potentially lower future revenues. Nevertheless, historical resilience and adaptability underscore the likelihood of mitigating adverse impacts through strategic adjustments and diversification efforts.

   # Financial Outlook  
   In light of the above analysis, the outlook remains cautiously optimistic. Although free cash flow may weaken further, leveraging existing assets and optimizing capital allocation strategies can mitigate negative effects. Continued emphasis on R&D investments and expanding international presence supports sustainable expansion plans. Market dynamics favoring digital transformation continue to drive demand for Apple products, offering opportunities for enhanced penetration rates and higher sales volumes. Therefore, the financial forecast anticipates modest but resilient growth prospects moving forward.

   # Investment Recommendation  
   Based on the comprehensive evaluation provided, Apple Inc. presents compelling investment opportunities characterized by substantial profitability, diversified product portfolio, and robust market positioning. Considering the favorable valuation relative to enterprise multiples and projected growth trajectories, prudent investors might consider allocating funds towards Apple shares. Nonetheless, thorough risk assessments remain essential to navigate potential fluctuations arising from macroeconomic conditions and industry-specific developments. Investors should exercise discretion when making decisions, recognizing the inherent volatility present in the tech sector.

March quarter records for total company revenue, iPhone revenue, and EPS
Services revenue reaches new all-time high
CUPERTINO, CALIFORNIA — Apple
®
today announced financial results for its fiscal 2026 second quarter ended March 28, 2026. The Company posted quarterly revenue of $111.2 billion, up 17 percent year over year. Diluted earnings per share was $2.01, up 22 percent year over year.
“Today Apple is proud to report our best March quarter ever, with revenue of $111.2 billion and double-digit growth across every geographic segment,” said Tim Cook, Apple’s CEO. “iPhone achieved a March quarter revenue record, fueled by such extraordinary demand for the iPhone 17 lineup. During the quarter, Services achieved yet another all-time record, and we were excited to introduce remarkable new products to our strongest lineup ever. That included the addition of the iPhone 17e and the M4-powered iPad Air, along with the launch of MacBook Neo, which is captivating customers all around the world.”
“Our strong business performance during the March quarter generated over $28 billion in operating cash flow and drove new March quarter records for both operating cash flow and EPS,” said Kevan Parekh, Apple’s CFO. “Continued strong customer demand for our products and services once again helped us achieve a new all-time high for our installed base of active devices across all major product categories and geographic segments.”
Apple’s board of directors has declared a cash dividend of $0.27 per share of the Company’s common stock, an increase of 4 percent. The dividend is payable on May 14, 2026, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on May 11, 2026. The board of directors has also authorized an additional program to repurchase up to $100 billion of the Company’s common stock.
Apple will provide live streaming of its Q2 2026 financial results conference call beginning at 2:00 p.m. PT on April 30, 2026, at apple.com/investor/earnings-call. The webcast will be available for replay for approximately two weeks thereafter.
Apple periodically provides information for investors on its corporate website, apple.com, and its investor relations website, investor.apple.com. This includes press releases and other information about financial performance, reports filed or furnished with the SEC, information on corporate governance, and details related to its annual meeting of shareholders.
